blocks.content:
**What you'll learn:** A quick tour of what’s new and better in Grafana 13. It all runs on Play, so you don’t need your own stack to follow along.

Each stop jumps you to a real example, where you can open it, click around, and see how the feature behaves in an actual UI.
Curious to see how our Business Intelligence dashboards are enhanced by a semantic layer in Grafana? Check out the interactive guide!
Grafana SQL expressions let you use SQL to query and transform panel query results on the server after those queries run, and before the panel renders.
**SQL Expressions Showcase** — Open this dashboard to try SQL expressions hands-on.
Add this SQL expression to filter values above 1.33
Click the **Run query** button
Congratulations! You've written your first SQL expression with Grafana!

Grafana has revamped the Gauge visualization!

The new gauge introduces a Circular style with built-in sparklines, gradients, and accessibility improvements, such as Center glow.
**Gauges** — Open this dashboard to try the new and improved **Gauge** visualization.
Click the **Circular** button to change the style from arc to circular
Toggle **Center glow** to add a center glow effect
Increase the segment by adding a number

Congratulations! You've tried out the revamped Gauge visualization. To find out more what else we have changed, check out [Revamped gauge visualization]([https://grafana.com/whats-new/2026-01-19-revamped-gauge-visualization/).

Grafana has revamped the **Table** visualizations so dashboards look sharper and tell a clearer story.

The table visualization, which includes performance improvements, introduces new cell types: **Pill** and **Markdown + HTML** cells.
**Table** — Open this dashboard to explore the table visualization.
Follow along to try some of the new Table features

steps.reftarget: div[data-testid='data-testid Cell options Cell type field property editor'] input[role='combobox']
div[data-testid='data-testid Cell options Cell type field property editor'] input[role='combobox']
steps.description: Type **Pill** to change the cell type

blocks.content: Congratulations! You've tried out the revamped Table visualization. To find out more about what else we have changed, check out [New table visualization]([https://grafana.com/whats-new/2025-09-05-new-table-visualization-is-generally-available/]).

Styling panels in Grafana used to require a lot of manual work. Editing each panel individually, or duplicating one just to reuse its look, and then swapping out the query. 

With the new copy-paste styles feature, you can copy the styling from one panel and apply it to another in just a couple of clicks.
**Copy-paste styles** — Open this dashboard to try out the new copy-paste styles feature and follow the guided step below!
Follow along to see how copy-paste styles work in action!

steps.reftarget: div[data-testid='data-testid dashboard controls'] button[data-testid='data-testid Edit dashboard button']
steps.description: Click the **Edit** button if you are not in edit mode.

steps.reftarget: div[data-testid='data-testid header-container'] button[data-testid='data-testid Panel menu Gauge with sparkline and center glow']
steps.description: On this panel, hover and click the three-dot icon

steps.reftarget: div[data-testid='data-testid portal-container'] div[data-testid='data-testid Panel menu item Styles']
div[data-testid='data-testid SubMenu container'] button[data-testid='data-testid Panel menu item Copy styles']
div[data-testid='data-testid header-container'] button[data-testid='data-testid Panel menu Gauge with sparkline and gradient']
steps.description: On a different panel, hover and click the three-dot icon

steps.reftarget: div[data-testid='data-testid portal-container'] div[data-testid='data-testid Panel menu item Styles']
div[data-testid='data-testid SubMenu container'] button[data-testid='data-testid Panel menu item Paste styles']

blocks.content: Congratulations! You've tried out the new copy-paste styles feature!

Visualization Suggestions have been updated to provide more relevant suggestions and are now the default method for selecting a panel visualization.

When you preview a visualization using the updated Suggestions feature, we give you a full-sized preview of the suggestion in the panel editor before you select the suggestion.
**London Transport TFL** — Open this dashboard, then follow the interactive steps below to try the new and improved visualization suggestions.
Click the **Change** button to select a different visualization type.
Select one of the suggested visualizations and see your visualization update!
